source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: Conducting Human-Computer Interaction Scientific Experiments in Extreme Environments: Insights from Analog Mars Missions
paper.abstract: HCI experiments in extreme environments. Mars Desert Research Station missions. Longitudinal UX study (12 days). Indoor/outdoor experiments. User Experience Questionnaire. Friedman ANOVA analysis. N=8 participants.

verdict.summary: Legitimate HCI research with empirical methodology. Statistical analysis (Friedman ANOVA, chi-square) is standard and necessary. Acknowledges limitations (sample size, transferability). No formalism theater.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** HCI experiments in extreme environments
**Level:** 2-3 (Empirical HCI research)
**Rationale:** Novel application of UX methodology to Mars analog missions.

### Necessity Test
- Chi-square tests: Standard statistical reporting - NECESSARY
- UEQ formulas: Standard methodology - APPROPRIATE
- No set theory for simple operations

**Verdict:** Standard empirical research methodology.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Intelligent Expert Systems Using Molecular Fuzzy Genetic Algorithms and Multi-Objective Particle Swarm Optimization for Circular-Oriented Project Investments
paper.abstract: Decision-making model for circular-oriented project investments. Molecular fuzzy sets combining molecular geometry and fuzzy logic. Information gain for project prioritization. Q-learning for expert evaluation. Genetic algorithms for criteria weighting. Swarm optimization for ranking.

verdict.summary: Multi-criteria decision-making with novel molecular fuzzy sets. Equations (1-5) define entropy, information gain, Q-learning, and optimization - necessary for the proposed methodology. Heavy formalism appropriate for Level 3 optimization.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** MCDM with molecular fuzzy optimization
**Level:** 3 (Novel optimization methodology)
**Rationale:** Proposes new molecular fuzzy sets and integration with GA/PSO.

### Necessity Test
- Entropy equation (Eq. 1): Necessary for information gain
- Q-learning update (Eq. 3-5): Necessary for learning algorithm
- Optimization formulas: Necessary for methodology

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for optimization methodology.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Design of a Microcomputer for Controlling a Servo System for Cutting Steel Pipes
paper.abstract: Automatic regulation of steel pipe cutting. Microcomputer-based control for servo system. Hydraulic servo dynamics. Improved accuracy over existing systems. Production line synchronization.

verdict.summary: Control systems engineering for industrial application. Differential equations (Eq. 1-2) describe servo system dynamics - standard and necessary for control design. Domain-appropriate formalism.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Servo system control for pipe cutting
**Level:** 3 (Control systems engineering)
**Rationale:** Novel microcomputer-based control implementation.

### Necessity Test
- Motion equation (Eq. 1-2): Necessary for servo dynamics
- Control system analysis: Standard engineering

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for control systems.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Effect of Random Variation of the Initial Values of Ordinary Differential Equations and its Web-based Application to Chemical Kinetics
paper.abstract: Monte Carlo analysis of ODE initial value sensitivity. Chemical kinetics application (ABC reaction, furfural). Web-based computing. Gaussian variation of initial concentrations. Python implementation.

verdict.summary: Applied mathematics with web implementation. ODEs (Eq. 1-2) for chemical kinetics are standard and necessary. Monte Carlo methodology well-justified. Working web pages provided for verification.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Monte Carlo analysis of ODEs
**Level:** 3 (Applied mathematics)
**Rationale:** Sensitivity analysis with web-based implementation.

### Necessity Test
- Chemical kinetics ODEs (Eq. 1-2): Necessary for the problem
- Monte Carlo methodology: Standard approach

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for applied mathematics.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Problem Setting for Trajectory Planning and Cruise Control of a Connected Autonomous Electric Bus in Intersection Scenarios with Human-Driven Vehicles to Optimize Energy, Comfort and Tracking
paper.abstract: Two-stage design for CAEB trajectory planning. Energy consumption and passenger comfort optimization. Traffic light model. Lane-changing coordination. V2I communication. Kinematic vehicle model.

verdict.summary: Autonomous vehicle control optimization. Set definitions (Eq. 1-4) and kinematic models are standard and necessary for trajectory planning. Heavy formalism appropriate for Level 3-4 control optimization.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** CAEB trajectory planning
**Level:** 3-4 (Control systems optimization)
**Rationale:** Novel two-stage design for autonomous bus.

### Necessity Test
- Route set Or (Eq. 1): Necessary for problem definition
- Traffic light model (Eq. 4): Novel, necessary for planning
- Vehicle kinematics: Standard control formulation

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for trajectory optimization.

### Issues
None identified.

batch_notes: Volume 28 Issue 3 (2025) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **No formalism theater detected.** All 5 papers have domain-appropriate formalism: HCI experiments (Vatavu - legitimate empirical research), MCDM optimization, servo control, applied mathematics with Monte Carlo, autonomous vehicle planning. Final: 0 CRITICAL, 0 LIMITED, 4 ADEQUATE, 1 STRONG.
